class: DIAGNOSTIC (harness/substrate feasibility — NOT a C1 corruption datapoint)
question: is city->country a viable true-scale C1 substrate (high N at high concentration, real native knowledge)?

subject_token_census.note: bulk multi-token (3-4 tok); single-token caps at 115

wall_1: multi-token city subjects -> key collinearity -> ill-conditioned AlphaEdit solve -> dW blow-up (8900 vs 207) -> model emits garbage. 2516/2631 of the pool unusable.
wall_2: strong-prior counterfactual underexpression: even single-token-subject city->country counterfactuals express only 27-42% (<85% anchor gate) -> under-editing confound -> invalid for a corruption study. The R5/R13 counterfactual-over-prior fragility.

cost_anchor.implication: a 100-240-run powered grid = 4-10 days on one 4090 (moot: substrate broken)

conclusion: city->country NOT viable. True-2000-scale single-relation high-concentration C1 with REAL native knowledge remains substrate-gated: needs single-token-value + clean(<=2-tok)-subject + EXPRESSIBLE-counterfactual + cardinality>=2000 + native-knowledge simultaneously; clean-subject single-token relations cap ~78-118 (country->capital 78, element->symbol ~118); high-cardinality relations have multi-token subjects + strong priors. The 78-entity country pool remains the clean ceiling (C1-(a) at N<=100).
advisor: pilot-first + cheap-diagnostic phase (advisor-steered) prevented a multi-day grid on a broken substrate; N=2000 collapse is weight-destruction (all-!), NOT compaction corruption -> not a C1 finding.
